steps.title: Use the tangent ratio
steps.content:
The value 
$$
\tan B=\frac{3}{4}
$$ means the side opposite angle $B$ and the side adjacent to angle $B$ are in this ratio:
$$
3:4
$$

steps.title: Find the larger scale
steps.content:
In the larger right triangle, $BC=15$ is the hypotenuse of the corresponding ratio.
$$
3:4:5
$$
The scale factor is
$$
\frac{15}{5}=3
$$
So the adjacent side is
$$
4(3)=12
$$

steps.title: Find the smaller height
steps.content: Subtract $DA=4$ from the adjacent side to get $BD$. $$ BD=12-4=8 $$ With the same $3:4$ ratio, $$ \frac{DE}{BD}=\frac{3}{4} $$ $$ DE=\frac{3}{4}(8)=6 $$ The answer is 6.
